title: elaeodendron pininsulare subsp. poyaense
intro: Elaeodendron pininsulare subsp. poyaense is a subspecies of Elaeodendron pininsulare, a flowering plant native to the island of Madagascar. It is also known as Poya tree or Poya or Poya wood tree. The Poya tree has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, making it useful in treating various health conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The herbal preparations of Elaeodendron pininsulare subsp. poyaense include infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
